metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.finding: S3's (94f9) file_impact metadata lists 'src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/config.py' and 'src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_config_schema.py' as edit targets, directly contradicting the story description body which states 'No src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/config.py change and no new rebar_reconciler/_config_schema.py — the real config home is the app-level src/rebar/_config_schema.py.' An implementer following the file_impact would edit the wrong files.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the two wrong file_impact entries with: {path: 'src/rebar/_config_schema.py', reason: 'Add backend field to ReconcilerConfig + _as_choice coercer in reconciler coercer table'}.

findings.finding: S3's file_impact list includes 'src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/config.py' (reason: 'Add reconciler.backend key, default jira') and 'src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_config_schema.py' (reason: 'Static-frozenset validation of backend key') — but the S3 description body explicitly states: 'No src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/config.py change and no new rebar_reconciler/_config_schema.py — the earlier draft named both in error.' The contradictory file_impact entries will cause an implementer following the file list to open and edit the wrong files.
findings.location: S3 (94f9-a9a0-7475-4e75) file_impact list vs. description body
findings.suggested_fix: Replace S3 file_impact entries: remove `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/config.py` and `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_config_schema.py`; add `src/rebar/_config_schema.py` with reason 'Add backend field to ReconcilerConfig + _as_choice coercer in reconciler coercer table'.

findings.finding: Parent AC4 states 'all ~29 test patch strings are migrated with no re-export shims' as a measurable deliverable, but child dfb9 explicitly states the '~29 mock.patch strings' count is wrong — there are zero mock.patch string patches — and reclassifies the test surface into three categories with different counts (~23 object-level patches unaffected, ~18 path-constant files, ~18 dotted-import files). The parent metric cannot be satisfied as written because the child contradicts its own unit of measure.
findings.location: child dfb9-d2d1-455d-48d9, Acceptance Criteria AC4 of parent epic, and dfb9 Testing/Scope sections
findings.suggested_fix: Update parent AC4 to read: 'the category-2 (~18 path-constant) and category-3 (~18 dotted-import) test targets are rewritten to adapters.jira.* paths atomically with no re-export shims — verified by grep returning zero hits for old root paths (excluding path-agnostic patch.object sites).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the return type and fallback behavior of `IdentityConvention.parse()` for non-rebar-id labels (e.g., `Optional[RemoteRef]` returning `None` on no-match) in the S2 Protocol definition, and add a contract-suite test case that passes a plain non-rebar-id label and asserts the expected return value.
evidence: S2 description: 'parses that label back to a RemoteRef (the single source of the format those four sites currently hand-inline)'
S2 contract suite: 'stamp a RemoteRef → label string; parse label string → RemoteRef; round-trip' — only the happy-path round-trip is specified; no test for a non-matching label
S2 context: 'the read is the "rebar-id:"/"rebar-id-" prefix scan at binding_walk.py:341' — the scan encounters all ticket labels, not only rebar-id ones, so the parser must handle non-matching labels
ABSENCE: no mention in any story of Optional[RemoteRef] return type, None-on-no-match, or parse-raises-on-mismatch behavior for the IdentityConvention interface
findings.finding: The IdentityConvention Protocol's parse direction has an unspecified fallback: the plan describes 'parse label string → RemoteRef' but never specifies the return type or behavior when the parser encounters a label that is NOT a rebar-id label (e.g., a plain Jira label like 'team:backend'). Since the four inlined call sites do a prefix scan across all labels on a ticket, the parser will routinely receive non-matching inputs. Whether parse() returns Optional[RemoteRef] (None on no-match), raises, or returns a sentinel is a decision rule that must be co-located with the Protocol definition but is absent from the plan.
findings.location: S2 (ce4d-c3ca-5412-4798) — _backend.py IdentityConvention Protocol description; contract suite spec
scenarios: An implementor writing IdentityConvention.parse() must choose between Optional[RemoteRef] (None on no-match), raising ValueError, or returning a sentinel — the plan gives no guidance, so two implementors could make incompatible choices that both pass the specified round-trip test.
The contract suite as specified would pass even if parse() raises on non-rebar-id labels, which would break the binding_walk.py prefix-scan usage that calls it on every label.
findings.suggested_fix: In S2's _backend.py IdentityConvention Protocol definition, pin the parse signature as `def parse(self, label: str) -> Optional[RemoteRef]` with the rule 'returns None if the label does not match the rebar-id format'. Add one contract-suite test: `assert backend.identity.parse("team:backend") is None`. This matches the current binding_walk.py prefix-scan pattern and makes the no-match behavior unambiguous.

evidence: 1 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly address the outbound_differ.py re-export/test-patch atomicity gap: either include the migration of test patch strings that go through `outbound_differ.<name>` re-exports in S4's scope (so the neutrality gate and green suite are simultaneously satisfiable), or replace the deleted outbound_fields re-exports with adapter-path re-exports as a transitional step within S4.
evidence: outbound_differ.py:63: 'from rebar_reconciler.outbound_fields import (  # noqa: F401\n    _LOCAL_TO_JIRA_TYPE, _assignee_matches, _diff_fields, _extract_jira_field, _map_local_to_jira_fields)' — comment says 'so outbound_differ.<name> keeps resolving for the field-diff test suite'
S4 AC: 'None of the 12 rewired core modules import adapters.jira.* or the root vendor modules... verified by the neutrality grep/test gate returning zero hits'
S5 description: 'the deduped category-2/3 test targets reference the new adapters.jira.* paths — verified by grep for the old targets returning zero hits'
The re-exports in outbound_differ.py are precisely the test-seam that category-3 tests use — removing them in S4 (mandatory for the neutrality gate) breaks those tests before S5 can fix them
findings.finding: S4's neutrality gate requires removing outbound_differ.py's noqa:F401 re-exports of outbound_fields symbols (the very imports that 'keep outbound_differ.<name> resolving for the field-diff test suite'), but the test patch strings that depend on those re-exports are not migrated until S5. This creates an atomicity gap: after S4 lands, the field-diff tests that patch through outbound_differ.<name> break, with no fix until S5 lands.
findings.location: S4 scope/AC ('Changes callers, not file locations') vs. outbound_differ.py:63-69 (# noqa: F401 re-exports) vs. S5 scope ('migrate ~29 patch strings atomically')
scenarios: S4 lands: neutrality gate forces removal of outbound_differ.py's outbound_fields re-exports; field-diff test suite tests that patch rebar_reconciler.outbound_differ._map_local_to_jira_fields immediately fail.
S4 tries to keep the re-exports to avoid breaking tests: neutrality gate fires (outbound_differ.py still imports root vendor module outbound_fields), blocking merge.
findings.suggested_fix: In S4's scope, add: 'For each noqa:F401 re-export in outbound_differ.py that is removed by the neutrality gate, simultaneously rewrite the test patch strings that resolve through outbound_differ.<name> to patch the new Backend adapter path — these are category-3 patches that must move with the import, not wait for S5.'

findings.finding: Parent AC-7 states 'adding a backend requires only a new adapters/<x>/ implementing the pinned interface — verified by the proof-of-seam FakeBackend passing the contract suite with no core edit,' but S3 (94f9) design requires editing _config_schema.py (a core app-level config file, not adapters/<x>/) to add the new key to the _as_choice static set for every new backend.
findings.location: child 94f9-a9a0-7475-4e75 — Approach ('A second backend adds its key to both the _as_choice set and the registry'); parent Acceptance Criteria AC-7
findings.suggested_fix: Option A (update AC-7): Revise parent AC-7 to read 'adding a backend requires implementing adapters/<x>/ and adding its key to the _as_choice set in _config_schema.py and the registry.' Option B (redesign S3): derive the _as_choice allowed-set dynamically from the populated registry at config-load time rather than hardcoding it, so only the adapters/<x>/ addition is needed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a documentation-audit step to S5: enumerate and update any READMEs, internal guides, or usage examples that reference the pre-migration paths (`acli*`, `adf`, `outbound_fields`, `comment_limits` at the package root).
evidence: ABSENCE: the plan mentions migrating ~29 test patch strings but does not mention auditing docs/READMEs/examples for stale import-path references
'no re-export shims' means old paths become dead immediately after the merge
T7 criterion: 'The change doesn't strand stale references (deleted/renamed artifacts still referenced)'
findings.finding: S5 removes files from their current package-root locations with no mention of auditing or updating any existing documentation, READMEs, usage examples, or internal guides that reference the old import paths (e.g., `rebar_reconciler.acli`, `rebar_reconciler.adf`).
scenarios: A contributor follows a README or internal wiki example that imports from the old path and gets an ImportError with no guidance
Existing architecture docs or onboarding guides reference old module locations, misleading future backend implementors

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the default for opt-in capability Protocols in the Design section (e.g. 'initial implementation ships zero opt-in protocols; first candidate is X') so the S1 author has a concrete starting point and S2 has no ambiguity.
evidence: 'Five role Protocols (TicketTransport, OutboundMapper, InboundMapper, FieldSanitizer, IdentityConvention) behind one Backend facade, + opt-in capability Protocols.'
S1 is 'docs only' but must decide the full interface including opt-in capabilities — yet no opt-in protocols are named or even exemplified in the epic.
findings.finding: The Design section names five role Protocols but introduces 'opt-in capability Protocols' without enumerating or naming any of them. An agent executing S1 (which pins the full interface in ADR 0035 §(d)) must invent these capabilities with no guidance, creating an undecided design choice at epic level.
findings.location: Design section — 'Five role Protocols … + opt-in capability Protocols'; S1 story description
scenarios: An agent executing S1 to write ADR 0035 §(d) has no basis for deciding which optional capability protocols to include, risking either an overly thin interface (zero opt-ins) or an inconsistent one across S1→S2.
An S2 implementer may implement a different set of opt-ins than S1 specified, requiring a rework loop.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence such as: 'Opt-in capability Protocols (initial set: none; the first anticipated addition is a BulkFetch capability for backends that support batch retrieval — tracked in epic be74).' This gives S1 a concrete floor to document and S2 a known contract surface.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace '~29' with either the exact count (once confirmed) or a deterministic completeness check (e.g., 'a grep/ast scan for patch strings referencing the pre-move module paths returns zero hits') so the criterion is unambiguously evaluable.
evidence: 'migrate the ~29 test patch strings atomically (no shims)' — the tilde signals the author is uncertain of the exact count.
No secondary deterministic signal is provided to confirm completeness independently of the count (e.g., 'grep for remaining root-module patch strings returns zero').
findings.finding: The S5 acceptance criterion references '~29 test patch strings' with an approximation marker ('~'), making the criterion slightly non-deterministic: an executor who finds 27 or 31 strings cannot tell whether they are done or have missed some.
findings.location: AC bullet for S5 — 'all ~29 test patch strings are migrated with no re-export shims'
scenarios: An agent migrating patch strings stops at 27 (within the ~29 margin) but two remain, leaving a latent import path that breaks once the root shim is removed in a follow-on.
make test could still pass with residual old-path patches if the shim is present, masking the miss.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the AC to: 'all test patch strings referencing the pre-move root paths (currently ~29; exact count pinned in S5) are migrated — verified by `grep -r "patch.*acli\|patch.*adf\|patch.*outbound_fields\|patch.*comment_limits" tests/` returning zero hits under the old paths.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the S4/S5 boundary contradiction: either (a) remove the 'move ADF-sizing…' clause from the S4 decomposition body (physical relocation stays exclusively in S5) and clarify that S4 only inlines those values through the Backend interface, or (b) add an explicit AC bullet for S4 covering the physical move and adjust the S5 AC accordingly.
evidence: S4 body: 'move ADF-sizing + lossy-status/parent + duplicated maps/link-constants into `adapters/jira/`'
Boundary statement: 'S4 = who-calls-what (route all consumers, core neutrality), S5 = where-files-live (physical move)'
AC bullet 3 covers only: 'neutrality gate on the 12 rewired core sites' — no mention of physical relocation
findings.finding: The S4 decomposition body says 'move ADF-sizing + lossy-status/parent + duplicated maps/link-constants into `adapters/jira/`' — a physical relocation — but the plan's own boundary statement declares 'S4 = who-calls-what … S5 = where-files-live (physical move)' and the S4 AC covers only a neutrality gate on 12 rewired call-sites. This creates a cross-section contradiction (what S4 does vs. what the S4/S5 boundary says) and an E1 gap (a physical-move deliverable described in S4 has no covering AC criterion in S4).
findings.location: Child decomposition §S4 ('move ADF-sizing + lossy-status/parent + duplicated maps/link-constants into adapters/jira/') vs. boundary summary ('S4 = who-calls-what … S5 = where-files-live') and AC bullet 3
scenarios: An implementer reading S4's description will move files in S4; an implementer reading the boundary statement and AC will not — they land in two different edit-sets.
If the physical move happens in S4, the S5 AC ('reside under adapters/jira/') becomes vacuously true before S5 starts, undermining S5's independently-reviewable contract.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the phrase 'move ADF-sizing + lossy-status/parent + duplicated maps/link-constants into `adapters/jira/`' from the S4 decomposition body and replace with 'expose ADF-sizing, lossy-status/parent, and duplicated maps/link-constants *through the Backend interface* (the physical files move in S5)' — keeping S4 strictly in the 'who-calls-what' domain.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define what 'neutrality gate' means in the plan — e.g., 'a CI step running `importchecker` / `import-linter` with a rule forbidding `adapters.jira` imports in `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/`' — so the AC is unambiguously verifiable.
evidence: AC: 'enforced by a neutrality gate on the **12 rewired core sites**'
ABSENCE: the term 'neutrality gate' does not appear in Design, decomposition body for S4, or anywhere else in the plan
findings.finding: The AC bullet 3 mandates a 'neutrality gate on the 12 rewired core sites' as the verifiable S4 outcome, but neither the Design section, the S4 decomposition body, nor any other plan section defines what artifact or mechanism constitutes this gate (e.g., an import-graph CI check, a lint rule, a test fixture). The criterion cannot be verified without knowing what the gate is.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria bullet 3: 'enforced by a neutrality gate on the 12 rewired core sites'
scenarios: An implementer may implement any arbitrary check and claim it satisfies the AC, or may skip it entirely because the mechanism is unspecified.
Reviewers cannot verify the AC is met without an agreed definition.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Strengthen the S5 AC to explicitly verify removal: add a clause such as 'and the originals are deleted from the package root (no file at the old path remains)' alongside the 'reside under adapters/jira/' assertion.
evidence: AC bullet 4 states only where files *reside*, not that they are *absent* from the root
'no re-export shims' prevents aliasing but does not assert root deletion
E1 criterion: 'for migrations, criteria verify BOTH removal and replacement'
findings.finding: The S5 AC bullet verifies replacement ('reside under `adapters/jira/`') but does not explicitly verify *removal* from the package root. For a migration, both sides should be verifiable. The only implicit removal signal is 'no re-export shims', which rules out shimmed aliases but not an outright copy that leaves originals in place.
scenarios: A passing implementation could leave the originals at the root (importing from two locations) and still technically satisfy 'reside under adapters/jira/' and 'no shims'.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC bullet 4: '…and the corresponding root-level files (`acli*`, `adf.py`, `outbound_fields.py`, `comment_limits.py`) no longer exist at the package root.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit back-out / rollback plan for S5 (and the S4→S5 chain): e.g., a git-revert strategy, a temporary re-export shim window, or a feature-flag gate that can be toggled — the 'full suite green' gate is a prevention measure, not a recovery mechanism.
evidence: 'physically relocate `acli*`/`adf`/`outbound_fields`/`comment_limits` into `adapters/jira/`'
'all ~29 test patch strings are migrated with no re-export shims (S5)'
AC only states 'Full suite green at every landed change' as a forward quality gate — no back-out plan is described
T4 criterion: 'The remedy is an explicit rollback/back-out plan or expand-contract — acknowledgement alone is insufficient'
findings.finding: The S5 atomic file relocation (acli*/adf/outbound_fields/comment_limits) and ~29 test-patch-string migration are explicitly irreversible (no-shim policy), but the plan provides no explicit rollback or back-out path if the migration causes a regression mid-chain.
findings.location: S5 (`dfb9-d2d1-455d-48d9`) description and AC: 'migrate the ~29 test patch strings atomically (no shims)'
scenarios: S5 lands, a subtle test-patch miss is discovered post-merge; no documented back-out path means the team must ad-hoc reverse a broad rename
S4 rewires all 12 core sites; a production regression is found; the strict S1→S5 chain has no described partial-rollback procedure
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback / Back-out' subsection to S5 (and optionally S4) stating: (a) the back-out is a git revert of the S5 commit(s), (b) the conditions under which a revert would be triggered, and (c) that the no-shim policy holds because the revert is the stated rollback path — making the choice explicit and the mechanism named.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify and update the user-facing config reference (README config section, schema file, or docs page) to document `config.reconciler.backend`, its type, default value (`"jira"`), and valid keys as part of S3's definition of done.
evidence: 'add `reconciler.backend` config (static-key validated) + an in-tree registry + `select_backend()`'
No mention of a config-reference doc update in S3's description or the AC
T7 criterion: 'A new pattern/contract/config/CLI gets a doc/ADR'
findings.finding: The new `config.reconciler.backend` config key introduced in S3 is described only in the implementation steps; the plan does not identify a user-facing config-reference doc (e.g., config schema docs, README config section) that should be updated to document the new option and its valid values.
scenarios: A developer or operator wishing to configure a non-default backend cannot find the option documented anywhere except by reading source code
When epic be74 ships a second backend, the config key is already undocumented, making the feature invisible to users

findings.finding: Parent AC-4 states 'all ~29 test patch strings are migrated with no re-export shims (S5)' but S5 (dfb9) explicitly contradicts this, saying 'the first draft's ~29 mock.patch strings was wrong; there are zero mock.patch string patches' — replacing the concept with categories 2 (~18 path-constant files) and 3 (~18 dotted-import files). The parent AC uses erroneous terminology that S5 does not reconcile upward.
findings.location: child dfb9-d2d1-455d-48d9 — Context/Problem ('characterized correctly — the first draft's ~29 mock.patch strings was wrong'); parent Acceptance Criteria AC-4
findings.suggested_fix: In parent AC-4, replace 'all ~29 test patch strings are migrated with no re-export shims' with 'the category-2 (~18 path-constant) and category-3 (~18 dotted-import) test references are rewritten to adapters.jira.* paths (zero mock.patch string patches exist; the ~23 patch.object _load_acli tests are path-agnostic and verified still-green), with no re-export shims — per S5's corrected taxonomy.'

findings.finding: The three opt-in capability Protocols (`SupportsLinks`, `SupportsComments`, `SupportsIncremental`) are referenced in the plan with the rule that a backend is 'never asked to sync links' unless it advertises `SupportsLinks`, and detection is 'not via structural introspection' — but the positive detection mechanism is never specified anywhere in the plan. The contract suite AC (S2) says 'a capability Protocol's methods are exercised only when the backend advertises it (observed via behavior)' but gives no dispatch rule. This is a decision-rule gap: the processing protocol for capability detection is not co-located with the capability Protocol schema.
findings.location: S2 Testing section (ce4d-c3ca-5412-4798) / Design section of epic plan

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
